title: Challenge : Creating New EC2 Instance using Snapshot

tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click Open Console to launch the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab.
On the login page: Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit). Input the provided IAM Username and Password. Click Sign In.
Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit).
Input the provided IAM Username and Password.
Click Sign In.
In the Console, select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

XYZ Company wants to save a copy of its website server and use it to make more servers exactly the same. Your job is to start a virtual computer, save its storage, make a template from that, start a new computer with the template, and check if it works. Here’s how to do each step super simply:

Launch an EC2 Instance: Launch an instance with Amazon-linux-2023 kernel-6.1 image and t2-micro as instance type Use port 22, port 80 in security groups Under User data enter the script to: Install apache Create simple index.html file
Launch an instance with Amazon-linux-2023 kernel-6.1 image and t2-micro as instance type
Use port 22, port 80 in security groups
Under User data enter the script to: Install apache Create simple index.html file
Install apache
Create simple index.html file
Create a Snapshot from the Instance:
Create an AMI from the Snapshot:
Launch an EC2 Instance using Newly Created AMI: Launch the instance with the newly created image with t2-micro and same sg that used before
Launch the instance with the newly created image with t2-micro and same sg that used before
Test the New EC2 Instance Created from AMI: Copy public IP and check webpage working or not
Copy public IP and check webpage working or not
Check if everything is set up right. In the lab console, click Validate to make sure the instances, snapshot, and template are correct.
Sign out of the AWS Management Console.
In the lab console, click End Challenge and wait for it to finish.
You’ve completed the EC2 Snapshot challenge!
